page: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 8 shared vCPUs based on the AMD EPYC-Rome processor architecture running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 160 GB SSD, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a balanced performance profile. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2548.38, while multi-core performance falls in the average tier at 20252.78. Memory bandwidth tests show average capabilities, reaching 888.0 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 89.3 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 23.39 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation with a small 135M model is strong at 259.42 tokens/sec, while medium 7B model inference remains average.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the cx43 offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated, sustained CPU resources. The combination of 8 vCPUs and 160 GB of local SSD storage makes it suitable for multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, intermediate database operations, and lightweight text or image processing.
description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, this instance lacks dedicated GPU hardware and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU rating in the top 25% alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. While single-threaded gzip compression is relatively weak, the server demonstrates solid performance in software compilation, database operations, and small-model LLM text generation. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and shared CPU allocation make the cx43 a cost-effective option for workloads requiring moderate multi-threaded compute capacity without the premium of dedicated cores. It is well-suited for static web serving, software development builds, and lightweight data processing.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage, delivering balanced compute performance for general workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cx43 is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, managed via the QEMU hypervisor.
tagline: A cost-efficient virtual server featuring eight shared AMD EPYC cores and local SSD storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and small-model LLM text generation performance
QEMU hypervisor-based x86_64 architecture without dedicated GPUs
Best suited for software compilation, static web serving, and database operations
